behavior: Usually solitary; sits upright on low, exposed perches, repeatedly pumps its tail, and makes short sallies to catch flying insects, often returning to a nearby perch. Builds a mud-and-moss cup nest beneath a protective overhang.
field_marks: Sooty gray-brown head contrasting with a paler throat
Plain gray-brown back and wings with no strong adult wingbars
habitat: Open woodland, woodland edges, brushy clearings, stream margins, farms, and other semi-open settings with low perches; nests on sheltered rock ledges or beneath overhangs on structures.
